title: PII Exposure Scanner
assumptions: Primary scan scope used tracked files from `git ls-files`; untracked secret-prone filenames were additionally checked for ignore-gap and secret exposure risk.
This is static analysis only; no runtime traffic inspection or production data samples were available.
exclusions: Excluded `.git` internals, cache artifacts (`.pytest_cache`, `.ruff_cache`, coverage artifacts), and vendored/third-party code (none detected).
No hardcoded literal emails, phone numbers, SSNs, card numbers, DOB values, passport/license numbers, or structured full-person-name datasets were found in tracked source/config/test files.

findings.detail: The hook writes extracted query keywords and per-result title/summary snippets to `activity.log`. Those values are derived from user prompts and stored learning content, so names, emails, phone numbers, or other identifiers can be persisted in plaintext logs without redaction.
findings.recommendation: Stop logging raw query/snippet text; log opaque IDs/counts only. Add a centralized redaction step before any log write.

findings.detail: Subprocess stderr is appended directly to `activity.log` (`$(cat "$ERR_FILE")`). Error streams from transcript parsing/search paths may include user-derived content or identifiers and are not sanitized.
findings.recommendation: Log bounded error codes/messages instead of raw stderr; if stderr must be retained, apply PII masking before write.

findings.detail: Generated learning titles (transcript-derived) and indexer output are written to `activity.log`. If transcripts include personal identifiers, they can be reflected in titles/error output and logged unredacted.
findings.recommendation: Avoid logging transcript-derived titles/content; log file IDs plus status. Sanitize child process output before appending to logs.

findings.detail: Database schema stores raw `content` in `learnings` and duplicates it into `fts_learnings`; `upsert_document` writes plaintext content directly. Any PII present in learnings is stored unencrypted at rest.
findings.recommendation: Add at-rest encryption (for example SQLCipher or application-layer field encryption) and run a PII scrub/tokenization step before persistence and FTS indexing.

findings.detail: Transcript-derived learnings are written directly to markdown files in `${HOME}/.projects/learnings` and repo `.projects/learnings` with no masking/encryption guardrails.
findings.recommendation: Introduce pre-write PII filtering (mask/remove direct identifiers), support encrypted local storage options, and restrict file permissions for learning directories.

findings.detail: Indexer reads full markdown learning content and persists it as-is via `db.upsert_document`, propagating any personal data from files into SQLite plaintext storage.
findings.recommendation: Apply content sanitization before indexing and store sensitive fields encrypted/tokenized; keep only minimal searchable metadata in clear text.

findings.detail: Required ignore patterns for `.env*`, key material, credential/secrets files, and common data dumps were missing, increasing accidental commit risk for local secrets or PII exports.
findings.recommendation: Keep the added patterns (`.env*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `credentials.*`, `secrets.*`, `*.sql`, `*.csv`, `*.xlsx`) and use checked-in `.example` templates for expected local configs.
